title: The Titles That Time Forgot
summary: A family was forced into an emergency property sale because outdated property titles — never updated after a grandparent's death — triggered a legal quagmire during probate. What should have been a smooth inheritance became a six-figure loss with court fees, back taxes, and a forced below-market sale.
principle: Financial cost of administrative delay in estate planning
lesson: Estate documents are not 'set it and forget it.' Review titles, beneficiaries, and deeds every 3-5 years or after any major life event.

title: The Gap That Swallowed a Savings Account
summary: A homeowner discovered — after a catastrophic pipe burst — that their insurance policy excluded water damage from internal plumbing failures. The $47,000 repair bill wiped out their emergency fund and forced them into high-interest debt. The exclusion was buried on page 34 of the policy.
principle: The Gap Principle: no coverage until you verify exclusions
lesson: Never assume you're covered. Read every exclusion clause. Ask your agent to walk you through what is NOT covered — that list matters more than what is.

title: Full House, Empty Pockets
summary: A short-term rental operator celebrated 100% occupancy for three consecutive months — then discovered they'd netted a loss. Cleaning fees had crept up 40%, dynamic pricing software was underpricing weeknights, and a new city permit fee ate the remaining margin. Revenue was a vanity metric masking operational bleed.
principle: Occupancy is vanity; Net per Night is truth
lesson: Track Net Revenue per Available Night (NRevPAN), not occupancy rate. A 70% occupied property at premium rates beats 100% occupancy at breakeven.

title: The Perfect Setup That Wasn't
summary: A seasoned trader identified a textbook bull-flag pattern on crude oil futures. Every technical indicator confirmed the setup. He sized up to 3x his normal position. Overnight, an unexpected OPEC emergency meeting crashed prices 12%. The 'perfect' trade became a five-figure loss because he'd ignored macro tail risk in his position sizing.
principle: Risk-Adjusted Return: never ignore 1% tail risk
lesson: Technical setups exist inside macro contexts. Always ask: 'What event, however unlikely, could invalidate this thesis overnight?' Then size your position as if that event is coming.

title: The Deal That Wasn't a Deal
summary: A first-time investor bought a 'below-market' duplex at auction, celebrating the $40,000 discount off comparable sales. Post-purchase inspection revealed: foundation cracks ($28,000), knob-and-tube wiring ($19,000), and an unpermitted addition that required $15,000 to remediate or demolish. The 'deal' cost $22,000 more than a turnkey property down the street.
principle: True cost of a home is never just the price tag
lesson: Total Cost of Acquisition = Purchase Price + Deferred Maintenance + Compliance Costs + Opportunity Cost. Always budget 15-25% above purchase price for unknowns on distressed properties.
